fix: layout overlap, event listener leak, alt-screen redraw

- Use absolute positioning for terminal/welcome screen so they stack
  instead of flowing vertically in flex column (fixes dual button issue)

  removeAllListeners() — fixes multi-viewer listener leak and session
  switching corruption

  apps to redraw their full screen
- Send resize on client join to sync browser terminal size with PTY

Co-authored-by: Jordan <devaloi@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: update ADR-001 with Foundry agent client-side tools findings (#6)

Document findings from testing a Foundry-hosted agent with client-side
function tools (foundry_agent.py):

- Client-side function tools on Foundry agents work — the Agent Framework
  registers definitions, intercepts calls locally, and submits results
- Server-side MCP tool events (e.g. Foundry IQ) leak through the AG-UI
  stream, causing phantom tool_call_end errors that could not be resolved
- This is the primary reason the project uses Option 3 (Foundry agent as
  tool) rather than a Foundry-hosted orchestrator
- Added 'what would unblock' criteria for future re-evaluation
- Updated comparison matrix with new rows for these findings

Co-authored-by: Jacob Spoelstra <jaspoels@microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(m2): thin end-to-end slice — engine, headless mode, game loop

* feat(m2): thin end-to-end slice — engine, headless mode, game loop

Implements M2 (cryptd-7uw): the minimal slice that proves the full
L1 architecture is wired up and runnable end-to-end without LLM.

New packages:
- internal/engine: deterministic rules engine (NewGame, Move, Look)
  with typed errors NoExitError and LockedError
- internal/interpreter: RulesInterpreter maps text commands to
  EngineActions (go <dir>, look, quit and aliases)
- internal/narrator: TemplateNarrator produces fixed-template
  narrations for all event types
- internal/renderer: CLIRenderer reads stdin / writes stdout
- internal/game: Loop wires engine+interpreter+narrator+renderer;
  drives full pipeline until quit or context cancel

CLI:
- crypt headless --scenario <id>: runs headless mode; respects
  CRYPT_SCENARIO_DIR env var for scenario lookup path

Tests:
- Unit tests in all packages (no build tag)
- Integration tests in internal/game with adversarial fakes covering
  ctx cancel, channel close, quit event, narrate/render error paths
- E2E test (build tag e2e) compiles binary and runs scripted session
- Coverage ≥ 90% on all M2 packages (engine 93.8%, game 92.1%,
  narrator/renderer 100%, interpreter 95%)

Fixtures:
- testdata/scripts/minimal-run.yaml: M2 acceptance script

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(m2): address review — state corruption, quit narration, context, types

Addresses all issues surfaced by Cursor Bugbot review:

- engine: validate destination room before mutating GameState so a
  failed Move never leaves state corrupted (state-before-error-check)
- engine: hidden connections now return NoExitError (undiscoverable
  until revealed) instead of LockedError
- engine/doc.go: remove premature 'combat, inventory' claims
- game/loop: route renderer-emitted quit events through dispatch so
  quit narration ('Farewell, adventurer.') is rendered before exit
- game/loop: render quit narration after dispatch returns quit event
- game/loop: map LockedError -> 'locked_door' event, NoExitError ->
  'no_exit' event so narrators can give specific feedback
- narrator: add 'locked_door' and 'no_exit' template narrations
- renderer/cli: move stdin scan into goroutine (startOnce) so
  Render never blocks on input and ctx cancellation is honoured
- cmd/crypt: use filepath.Join for cross-platform path assembly
- cmd/crypt: rename usage strings from 'crypt' to 'cryptd'
- e2e: build binary as 'cryptd' matching documented binary name
- testdata/scripts: fix header comment (no --script flag exists)
- tests: remove unused errRenderer; add locked_door/no_exit/unknown
  dispatch coverage; use select+timeout in renderer tests

Coverage: engine 91.2%, game 97.6%, narrator/renderer 100% (total 91.6%)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(engine): hidden connections not leaked in exitList

exitList filtered over all room.Connections without checking type,
causing hidden connections to appear in LookResult.Exits and
MoveResult.Exits — contradicting the engine's own policy that hidden
connections are undiscoverable until revealed.

Now exitList skips entries where conn.Type == "hidden", consistent
with Move which already returns NoExitError for hidden directions.

Added tests:
- TestLook_HiddenConnectionsNotInExits: verifies north (hidden) absent
- TestMove_HiddenIsNoExit: verifies hidden direction returns NoExitError
Updated minimal.yaml: added secret_room with hidden north exit from entrance.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(game): propagate internal engine errors from dispatch instead of swallowing them as no_exit

NoExitError and LockedError are expected game conditions mapped to distinct
events. Any other error from Engine.Move indicates state/scenario corruption
and must propagate so callers can surface a diagnostic.

Added TestLoop_MoveInternalErrorPropagates to cover the new branch.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Clarify skillpm’s position relative to GitHub CLI/Claude plugin specs (#57)

Users asked what emerging GitHub CLI and Claude plugin specs imply for
skillpm. This PR clarifies that those specs are host/runtime extension
mechanisms, while skillpm remains focused on portable Agent Skills
packaging and orchestration.

- **Documentation: plugin-spec positioning**
  - Added a new section to `README.md`:
    - `How this relates to plugin specs (GitHub CLI / Claude Code)`
  - Added a matching section to `docs/index.md`:
    - `How this relates to plugin specs`
  - Both sections explicitly state:
    - plugin specs and Agent Skills are complementary, not replacements
- skillpm’s model is unchanged (`npm` for deps + `skills` for wiring +
`add-mcp` for MCP config)

- **Docs consistency**
- Kept language aligned across repository README and docs homepage so
users get the same answer regardless of entry point.

Example excerpt added:

```md
- Plugin specs define host-specific extension/runtime hooks.
- Agent Skills define portable instruction packages that can be reused across agent systems.

For skillpm, the model stays the same: npm for dependency management, plus `skills` and `add-mcp` for wiring and MCP configuration.
```

commit.message:
fix(setup): eliminate stat+readFile TOCTOU in worktree .git detection (#301)

* Initial plan

* fix(security): eliminate TOCTOU race in worktree detection (CodeQL js/file-system-race #20)

Replace the two-step stat+readFile pattern with a single readFile
call that distinguishes normal git repos (EISDIR error) from worktree
links (successful read) in one atomic operation.

Before:
  fs.stat(gitDir)           // check: is it a directory?
  fs.readFile(gitDir, ...)  // use: read the file (race window here)

After:
  fs.readFile(gitDir, ...) // attempt read directly
  catch EISDIR             // .git is a directory → normal repo
  catch other              // .git missing/inaccessible → not a git repo

The new approach uses a single syscall, closing the TOCTOU window
entirely. Logic is semantically identical: worktrees still parse the
gitdir pointer, normal repos still proceed with gitDir unchanged.

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
ci: add codecov.yml with carryforward flags

The CHANGELOG bot commits to main with [skip ci] after every CI run.
Codecov sees those as HEAD with no coverage report -> Missing Head Report.

Carryforward: when a commit has no report, Codecov carries the last
known coverage for the 'unittests' flag forward instead of erroring.
require_head: false stops it blocking PR comments for the same reason.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
